title: Non-record: Selective Freeze on Random Linear Maps — Why Freezing Gate+Up Beats Full Freeze + LoRA
author: himanshudongre

data.description: Selectively freezes only the MLP gate and up projections while keeping attention and down projections learnable.
Uses frozen base weights with low-rank adaptation for full-freeze comparison.
Uses frozen base weights with low-rank adaptation for full-freeze comparison.
Uses frozen base weights with low-rank adaptation for full-freeze comparison.

novel_contributions: Selective freezing of only gate+up MLP projections outperforms full freeze + LoRA/VeRA.
Shows that frozen gate+up projections can act as structural regularization while preserving learnable attention and down projections.
Demonstrates that a larger selectively frozen model can beat a smaller fully trained model under the same artifact budget.
Provides FineWeb-validated evidence that full freeze + LoRA is bottlenecked by frozen attention weights rather than adapter rank.
Introduces an artifact-normalized scaling perspective for competition submissions.
